description: Fix four foundational on-disk format incompatibilities between Rust CLI and Go CLI: deployment ID construction (omit label suffix when empty), label default (empty string instead of contract name), transaction ID format (tx-0x prefix), and artifact path (relative source path instead of absolute compiled-output path).

userStories.title: Fix Deployment ID Construction (Omit Label When Empty)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want deployment IDs to use the format namespace/chainId/ContractName when label is empty and namespace/chainId/ContractName:label when non-empty, so that on-disk IDs are byte-identical to the Go CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: generate_deployment_id("default", 31337, "Counter", "") returns "default/31337/Counter"
generate_deployment_id("default", 31337, "Counter", "v2") returns "default/31337/Counter:v2"
The register command's manual ID construction uses the same shared function (no duplication)
resolve_deployment() correctly resolves both ID formats (with and without label)
Unit tests for generate_deployment_id() cover both empty and non-empty labels
cargo check passes across the entire workspace

userStories.title: Fix Label Default to Empty String
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the label field to default to empty string when unspecified, so that deployment records match the Go CLI's convention of never defaulting label to the contract name or 'default'.
acceptanceCriteria: When treb register is called without --label, the resulting deployment has "label": ""
When treb run deploys a contract whose on-chain event has an empty label, the resulting deployment has "label": ""
When a label IS explicitly provided (via --label or on-chain event), it is stored as-is
A contract_display_name helper returns ContractName when label is empty, ContractName:label when non-empty
cargo check passes across the entire workspace

userStories.title: Fix Transaction ID Format to Use tx- Prefix
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all transaction IDs stored as "tx-0x<full_64_hex_chars>" so that the format matches the Go CLI's tx-prefixed convention exactly.
acceptanceCriteria: In the run pipeline, transaction_id is stored as "tx-0x<full_64_hex_chars>"
In the register command, transaction_id uses the same full-hash format: "tx-0x<full_hash>"
Transaction records created by hydrate_transactions() use the tx- prefixed ID as their map key
All references to transaction IDs (in Deployment.transactionId and Transaction.id) are consistent
cargo check passes across the entire workspace

userStories.title: Fix Artifact Path to Use Relative Source Path
userStories.description: As a developer, I want artifact.path to store the relative Solidity source path (e.g., "src/Counter.sol") extracted from forge artifact metadata, so that it matches the Go CLI's format instead of storing the absolute compiled artifact path.
acceptanceCriteria: artifact.path stores the relative source file path (e.g., "src/Counter.sol") for deployments created via treb run
The source path is extracted from forge artifact metadata (sourceName or equivalent field), not constructed from the artifact file path
If no artifact match is found, artifact.path is "" (empty string)
For treb register (which lacks artifact metadata), artifact.path remains ""
cargo check passes across the entire workspace

userStories.title: Update Lookup Index and ID Parsing for Both ID Formats
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the lookup index and deployment resolution to handle both new Go-compatible IDs (namespace/chainId/Name) and old Rust-style IDs (namespace/chainId/Name:label) so that existing .treb/ directories continue to work.
acceptanceCriteria: LookupIndex::build() correctly indexes deployments regardless of whether the ID has a label suffix or not
resolve_deployment() with query "Counter" resolves a deployment with ID "default/31337/Counter" (no label)
resolve_deployment() with query "Counter:v2" resolves a deployment with ID "default/31337/Counter:v2"
resolve_deployment() with query "default/31337/Counter" resolves by exact match
Querying by address still works correctly regardless of ID format
Loading a deployments.json with old-style IDs (e.g., "default/31337/Counter:Counter") does not crash
cargo check passes; unit tests added for resolution edge cases

userStories.title: Update Golden Files and Verify All Tests Pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all golden files regenerated with the corrected formats and all tests passing, so that the test suite validates the new on-disk format end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: All golden files under tests/golden/ are regenerated using UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test
The normalizer handles the new tx-0x<HASH> format consistently
cargo test passes with zero failures across the entire workspace (without UPDATE_GOLDEN)
cargo clippy passes with no new warnings

Spot-check at least 3 golden files confirming: deployment IDs without trailing colon when label is empty, "label": "" (empty string), "transactionId": "tx-0x..." format, and artifact.path as relative source path
cargo check passes across the entire workspace
